summary: User asks composer to load CSV rows and route them to two named sinks based on a row predicate. Tests gate-routing path; exercises the routes field on a transform/gate node and two distinct sinks. Authored 2026-05-06 as part of Tier 1.5 Step B fan-out — the URL-download-line-explode scenario only exercises a linear pipeline; this scenario probes whether the gate-routing failure shape is comparable.
opening_prompt: Please create a pipeline that loads /tmp/customers.csv and routes each row by the `status` column: rows where status equals 'active' should go to an `active` sink at /tmp/active.csv, everything else to an `inactive` sink at /tmp/inactive.csv.

red_criteria.passivity_phrases_note: Same passivity catch-list as url-download-line-explode. Anti-tail-offer rule applies across all scenarios.
build_failure_sentinels: i cannot mark this pipeline complete
red_criteria.build_failure_note: Server-injected from service.py:_build_runtime_preflight_message() on completion-claim with failed preflight.

green_criteria.must_have_outputs_min_note: Routing must produce two named sinks. One-sink solutions are RED.
